blocks.content: Want to know if your favorite television series is among the latest canceled TV shows? Consider this your all-encompassing list on renewed shows, when a series gets the axe, and which titles are being reworked as “anthologies” to squeeze every single drop of fan love (and awards) out of them.

How it works: Renewals are obvious: new season = yay! Cancellations are more complicated. When a network or streaming service announces that they’re not making another season of a show, we count that as canceled. Sure, some shows “come to a natural end,” but someone somewhere said “enough.” So to avoid debating the semantics of “canceled” versus “retired,” we’re just going to go with the former. There are also some instances, either due to scheduling conflicts or various delays, where the answer is “I dunno.” In those instances, we will leave the series as renewed and list the most recent season until we’re told otherwise.
Related: TV Premiere Dates 2026

Feel free to debate specific titles in the comments. But first: learn which series will live on, which were renewed and then canceled, which are switching channels and which have simply reached their expiration date.
Highlights
Apple TV Wants to Return to Widow’s Bay

The cursed island of Widow’s Bay will open for another season, as Apple TV has chosen to renew the horror-comedy series. Starring Matthew Rhys as the mayor of a New England island community haunted by its mysterious past, the Certified Fresh genre-bending show secured the renewal ahead of its season 1 finale, which airs on June 17. Creator, showrunner, and executive producer Katie Dippold stated that “Season 2 is about how everything is great on the island and there’s nothing to worry about.”
Netflix’s A Good Girl’s Guide to Murder to End with Season 3

Only a couple of weeks after Netflix dropped the entire second season of A Good Girl’s Guide to Murder, the streamer has announced that it has been renewed for a third, and final, season. It’s not necessarily bad news, however, as the third season is meant to adapt the third book in Holly Jackson’s novel trilogy and wrap up the story of amateur teenage detective Pip (Emma Myers). Whether or not Netflix has any plans to adapt Jackson’s prequel novella, set before the events of the first season, remains unknown.
HBO Confirms Euphoria Is Officially Done

The finale of Euphoria’s third season just aired on Sunday, May 31, but that same night, HBO confirmed that the episode also served as the series finale, as the show would not continue for a fourth season. After two Certified Fresh seasons (and two Certified Fresh specials), Sam Levinson’s dark drama centering on a high school circle of friends took a left turn with season 3, following up with the group five years after the end of season 2, and earned less than enthusiastic reviews. Season 3 concluded fairly dramatically, so it’s not too surprising to learn the series is over, but it was certainly a choice to wait until the finale aired to announce it.
Emily in Paris to End with Season 6

It was just back in January that we learned Netflix had renewed Emily in Paris for a sixth season, less than a month before season 5 had premiered on the streamer. Fast forward to May, and in the same announcement where Netflix revealed season 6 had begun filming in Greece, they also stated that it would be the series’ last season. While season 6 will begin in Greece, the story will also take Emily to Monaco before she returns to Paris, where it all began.
